compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Epic `850f` needs a place for a T2 semantic backend to plug in and be reported, without touching the
deterministic T1 lane or any consumer. Today `TIER_T2` exists in the closed tier vocabulary but is
never emitted, and member/dotted references abstain inline in `resolve.refute_absence`. This story
adds the opt-in, default-off seam and wiring; S3 adds the actual pyright backend behind it. Shipping
the seam separately keeps the seam change regression-safe and independently reviewable.

## What

- New module `src/rebar/grounding/semantic.py` (stdlib-only, import-clean) exposing:
  - `T2_BACKENDS: tuple[str, ...] = ()` initially — the closed set of selectable backend names. (S3
    adds `"pyright"`.) An unknown/empty selection fails open to disabled.
  - `refute_semantic(reference, *, repo_root, config, timeout=None, cache=None) -> dict | None` —
    returns a normalized `TIER_T2` evidence record (`refuted`/`abstain`) or `None` meaning "T2 declined
    to run; keep the caller's T1 record". With no registered backend it returns `None` (default-off).
    `cache` is a **caller-owned** optional mapping (mirrors T1's `index` param) for reusing one
    backend invocation across many references in a single review — no module-global mutable state, so
    no cross-thread concern.
  - `available_backends() -> list[dict]` — `[{name, available, version}]` for each name in
    `T2_BACKENDS`, each probed fail-open (empty while `T2_BACKENDS` is empty).
- Extend `GroundingConfig` + `load_config` in `resolve.py` with three `.rebar/grounding.toml`
  `[grounding]` keys (all default-off / safe):
  - `t2_enabled: bool = False`, `t2_backend: str | None = None`, `t2_timeout_seconds: float = 30.0`.
    Invalid types/values fail open to the default (never a raise), consistent with the existing loader.
- Wire escalation in `oracle.refute_absence` (the single consumer entry): after the T1/deps lane
  returns, if the record is an `abstain` on a `member`/dotted reference or a not-found bare
  `symbol`/`import`, call `semantic.refute_semantic(...)`; a returned `refuted@T2` replaces the T1
  abstain, otherwise the T1 record is returned unchanged. `resolve.refute_absence` is **not** modified
  (T2 stays out of the deterministic T1 lane).
- Extend `oracle._backend_availability()` to append `*semantic.available_backends()` so
  `grounding_info` reports T2 backends.

## Scope

In scope: `semantic.py`, the config keys, the `oracle` escalation + availability wiring, and unit
tests. Out of scope: the pyright backend itself and its extra/docs (S3). While `T2_BACKENDS` is empty
the seam is fully inert.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/grounding/semantic.py` exists exposing `refute_semantic`, `available_backends`, and
      `T2_BACKENDS`.
- [ ] `semantic.py` is stdlib-only: `tests/unit/grounding/test_non_adoption.py` still passes
      (the import-purity guarantee holds).
- [ ] `GroundingConfig`/`load_config` expose `t2_enabled` (bool, default False), `t2_backend`
      (str|None, default None), and `t2_timeout_seconds` (float, default 30.0).
- [ ] Each new config key fails open to its default on malformed input (a test feeds a malformed
      `.rebar/grounding.toml` and asserts the defaults, with no raise).
- [ ] `oracle.refute_absence` escalates member/dotted and not-found-symbol abstains to
      `refute_semantic`.
- [ ] `oracle.refute_absence` returns `refute_semantic`'s `refuted@T2` when produced, otherwise the
      unchanged T1 record.
- [ ] `resolve.refute_absence` is unmodified (verified by diff review — the T1 lane stays out of T2).
- [ ] `oracle._backend_availability()` includes the T2 backends reported by `available_backends()`.
- [ ] `grounding_info()` output validates against `grounding_info.schema.json` after the change.
- [ ] Default-off regression: a test in `tests/unit/grounding/` asserts that with `t2_enabled=False`
      (empty `T2_BACKENDS`) `oracle.refute_absence` returns the pre-change evidence for a member/dotted
      reference (still `abstain` at `TIER_T1`).
- [ ] A seam-dispatch test using an in-test fake backend (monkeypatched into `T2_BACKENDS` +
      `refute_semantic`) shows a `refuted@T2` record replaces the T1 abstain.
- [ ] That `refuted@T2` record validates against `grounding.schema.json`.
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit/grounding/` exits 0.
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0.
compiled_state.title: T2 grounding S2: pluggable semantic seam (refute_semantic + config + oracle escalation, default-off)

verify_commands.dd_text: grounding unit tests pass (incl default-off regression)
